description: Places no longer used for their original purpose. They may be abandoned, destroyed, or occupied by creatures, presenting dangers and opportunities for the bold.
locations: Abandoned Fortress
The walls still stand but the gates hang open. Something drove the garrison out in a hurry. Their belongings remain, untouched.
Toppled Statue
A stone giant lies face down in the weeds, one hand still reaching. The pedestal bears claw marks too large to be human.
Sundered Wall
A great wall split clean through as if struck by a god's fist. Wind howls through the gap, carrying whispers from the other side.
Shattered Obelisk
Black stone fragments jut from scorched earth. The runes still glow faintly at night, and animals refuse to cross the blast circle.
Ruined Castle
Ivy chokes the battlements and rain fills the great hall. Somewhere deep below, a door slams shut on its own, again and again.
Ghost Town
Doors creak on rusted hinges. Plates still sit on tables, meals half eaten. Footprints appear in the dust with no one to make them.
Overgrown Villa
Jasmine and thornvine have swallowed the columns whole. The gardens grew wild and hungry long ago. Petals drift through shattered windows.
Mothballed Armory
Racks of weapons stand oiled and ready behind a sealed door. Someone expected to come back for them. No one ever did.
Crumbling Mansion
Plaster rains from the ceiling with every footstep. The grand staircase groans underfoot. Portraits watch from the walls with eyes that follow.
Lost Library
Dust motes float in shafts of light between toppled shelves. Pages rustle without wind. The books here remember things best left forgotten.
Forgotten Citadel
Stone towers rise from the fog, their banners long rotted away. The drawbridge is down as if expecting visitors no one remembers inviting.
Flooded Courtyard
Dark water fills the yard waist deep, still and cold. Stone benches jut like islands. Something ripples the surface from below.
Deserted Battlefield
Broken weapons rust in the mud. Crows circle but never land. The ground is soft and the air smells of iron even after all these years.
Cracked Pyramid
A fissure runs from base to apex, leaking cold air that smells of dust and copper. The stones hum when touched.
Desecrated Shrine
Holy symbols lie smashed on the floor. The altar is stained dark. Whatever was worshipped here has been replaced by something that leaves no offerings.
Desecrated Tomb
The sarcophagus lid has been pried open and shoved aside. Grave goods litter the floor, but the body is gone. Scratches mark the inside of the lid.
Collapsed Temple
Broken columns lean against each other like exhausted soldiers. Incense still lingers in the rubble. Prayer echoes from nowhere.
Ruined Cathedral
The rose window is shattered but light still pours through in colored shards. Pigeons roost where the choir once sang. The organ groans in the wind.
Abandoned Watchtower
The beacon at the top has gone dark. Stairs spiral upward past empty rooms where maps still hang, marking dangers that have long since moved.
Crumbling Monastery
Silent halls where monks once chanted now echo only dripping water. The herb garden has gone feral, and something nests in the bell tower.
Derelict Observatory
The great dome has cracked open to the sky. Lenses and brass instruments lie scattered. Star charts on the walls show constellations that no longer exist.
Sunken Amphitheater
Stone seats descend into murky water where the stage once stood. Frogs sing where actors performed. Masks float face up in the shallows.
Broken Aqueduct
A stone channel arcs across the valley, sheared clean at its midpoint. Water still trickles from the severed end, feeding a green pool far below.
Ruined Lighthouse
The tower leans seaward, its lamp long dark. Waves crash through the ground floor at high tide. Ships still wreck on the rocks it once guarded.
Collapsed Mine Shaft
Timbers jut from a mound of rubble and loose earth. A draft of cold air escapes through the gaps, carrying the smell of deep stone.
Empty Granary
Massive wooden bins stand hollow and dry. Rat bones crunch underfoot. The silence here is the particular quiet of a place that once fed thousands.
Shattered Workshop
Tools lie where they fell among broken glass and spilled reagents. Scorch marks climb one wall. Whatever was being built here, the last experiment failed.
Defunct Mill
The great wheel has seized in the current. Flour dust coats everything in pale gray. Gears and chains hang motionless, waiting for a hand to restart them.
Overgrown Cemetery
Headstones lean at odd angles beneath choking ivy. The names have worn away. Fresh flowers appear on one grave each morning, left by no one.
Deserted Prison
Cell doors stand open down a long corridor. Scratched tallies cover every wall. The deepest cell is still locked from the outside, and something breathes within.
Fallen Tower
The tower lies on its side like a felled tree, rooms exposed to the sky. Furniture clings to walls that are now floors. Birds nest in the fireplace.
Abandoned Quarry
A great gouge in the hillside filled with still green water. Cut stone blocks sit stacked and forgotten. Echoes carry strangely in the bowl shaped pit.
Crumbled Gatehouse
The portcullis has rusted halfway down. Arrow slits stare like blind eyes. The murder holes above still hold something dark and dried.
Flooded Crypt
Water seeps through cracked stone, pooling around the tombs. Candles float on the surface, still lit. No one has been down here to light them.
Decimated Barracks
Rows of cots lie overturned. Personal effects spill from open footlockers. The mess hall table is set for a meal that was never served.
Forgotten Archive
Scrolls and ledgers fill shelves from floor to ceiling in the dark. The air is dry and tastes of old paper. Some records here were hidden on purpose.
Ruined Palace
Gold leaf peels from the walls in long curling strips. The throne sits crooked on its dais. Footsteps echo through ballrooms where no one dances.
Collapsed Arena
Sand and rubble fill the fighting pit. Spectator seats have caved inward like broken teeth. Old bloodstains darken the stones beneath the dust.
Desolate Manor
Shutters bang in the wind. The garden fountain is dry and cracked. A single candle burns in an upper window, though the house has been empty for years.
Broken Windmill
The sails hang in tatters, creaking with each gust. Grain spills from a cracked millstone. The wind sounds almost like words when it passes through the blades.
Empty Stables
Hay has rotted to black mush in the stalls. Bridles hang from hooks, stiff with age. Hoofprints press deep into the dirt, heading out and never returning.
Ruined Academy
Chalkboards still bear lessons no one finished. Desks are overturned and books lie open to pages marked with desperate notes in the margins.
Abandoned Marketplace
Empty stalls line a wide square where weeds push through the cobblestones. Faded awnings snap in the breeze. A merchant's scales still balance on a counter.
Crumbling Aqueduct
Moss covers the arches and water drips through widening cracks. The channel above still holds a trickle. Stone dust falls with every tremor.
Deserted Lighthouse
Salt wind scours the empty tower. The lens is intact but dark. The keeper's logs on the desk end mid-sentence, the last entry dated years ago.
Shattered Spire
A needle of stone broken halfway up, the top half nowhere to be found. Lightning scars spiral the remaining shaft. The air crackles before storms.
Overgrown Ruins
Vine and root have pulled the stonework apart over centuries. Trees grow through what were once rooms. Nature is patient and it is winning.
Sunken Plaza
Flagstones descend into dark water at the center of the square. Fish swim between submerged market stalls. Coins still glint on the fountain floor below.
Broken Drawbridge
Chains hang slack from the gatehouse, the bridge sagging into the moat. Rotting timbers groan underfoot. Crossing would be a gamble at best.
Collapsed Tunnel
Rubble chokes the passage but a narrow gap remains near the ceiling. Cold air flows through it. Something on the other side is digging toward you.
Ruined Watchtower
Half the wall is missing, giving a perfect view of the valley below. The warning bell has fallen from its mount and lies cracked on the floor.
Abandoned Brewhouse
Copper vats stand green with verdigris. The air is sour with old yeast. Barrels line the cellar walls, some still sealed and sloshing when touched.
Crumbling Amphitheater
Weeds crack through the tiered seating. The acoustics still carry a whisper from the stage to the highest row. Applause seems to echo from nowhere.
Empty Treasury
Vault doors hang open on massive hinges. Bare shelves line the walls. A single coin sits in the center of the floor, too perfectly placed to be forgotten.
Destroyed Siege Engine
A great wooden frame lies splintered across the field, iron fittings twisted and scorched. The stone it last hurled rests in a crater nearby.
Ruined Docks
Rotting pilings jut from the harbor like broken teeth. Ropes trail into green water. One ship remains, half sunk and listing, its cargo hold just above the waterline.
Forgotten Vault
A heavy door hidden behind a collapsed wall, still sealed. The lock is intricate and old. Scratches around the keyhole suggest many have tried and failed.
Collapsed Sewers
Cracked tunnels reek of stagnant water and old stone. Grates open into darkness below the streets. Something has been building nests from debris down in the deep.
Abandoned Foundry
Cold forges line the walls, anvils still in place. Slag heaps glitter with traces of strange metal. The bellows wheeze when the wind finds its way inside.
Ruined Inn
The sign still swings above the door. Tables and chairs sit ready for guests who stopped coming. Bottles behind the bar hold liquor older than anyone alive.
Crumbling Walls
A perimeter of stone that once held something in or something out. Gaps widen with each season. Whatever the walls were built to stop, the barrier is failing.
Derelict Ship
Barnacles coat the hull and the mast has snapped. The deck tilts at a sharp angle. Below decks, cargo crates remain lashed down, their contents unknown.
Sunken City
Rooftops and spires break the surface of a still lake. Fish swim through windows. On clear days the streets below are visible, and so are the shadows moving through them.
Abandoned Mine
Ore cart tracks vanish into a dark tunnel braced with rotting timber. Pickaxes lean against the wall. A lantern hangs near the entrance, still full of oil.
Ruined Bathhouse
Cracked tile mosaics line empty pools stained with mineral deposits. Steam still rises from a crack in the floor. The water below is warm and smells of sulfur.
Collapsed Keep
The roof has caved in, filling the great hall with rubble and sky. Walls still stand on three sides, offering shelter. Campfire ash suggests others found it useful too.
